assertions.expectation: Recommends increasing maxSurge significantly (e.g., 10-20 or higher) to parallelize node replacements
assertions.reason: Response recommends increasing maxSurge to 50% which is significantly higher than default of 1 and would parallelize upgrades

assertions.expectation: Explains that higher maxSurge means more nodes upgrading simultaneously but requires more compute quota
assertions.reason: While the response mentions increasing maxSurge for parallel upgrades, it does not explain the compute quota implications or requirements

assertions.expectation: Advises checking and requesting increased compute quota before the upgrade
assertions.reason: The response does not mention checking or requesting increased compute quota at all

assertions.expectation: Suggests staggering node pool upgrades or running non-conflicting pools in parallel
assertions.reason: Response includes a parallel upgrade strategy section with script showing how to upgrade pools in parallel batches

assertions.expectation: Mentions that maxUnavailable can be set >0 for non-critical pools to speed things up further
assertions.reason: Response sets maxUnavailable to 25% in examples and mentions balancing between speed and availability

assertions.expectation: Recommends expanding the maintenance window or splitting across multiple weekends
assertions.reason: The response focuses on technical solutions to fit within the existing 8-hour window but does not suggest expanding the window or splitting across weekends

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for updating surge settings on each pool
assertions.reason: Response provides gcloud container node-pools update command with --max-surge and --max-unavailable flags

assertions.expectation: Includes monitoring commands to track upgrade progress in real-time
assertions.reason: Response includes kubectl get nodes command with custom columns to monitor node versions and status, plus time command for upgrade duration
